description: EC-EDIT-014 (Cancel restores priorFocusedBlockId) test is tautological. The spec requires that activating Cancel from the save-failure banner causes the prior focusedBlockId to regain DOM focus (behavioral-spec REQ-EDIT-029, REQ-EDIT-023; verification-architecture PROP-EDIT-018: 'After editing snapshot returns, the prior focusedBlockId regains DOM focus'). The test only asserts `adapter.dispatchCancelSwitch.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)` — i.e., that the IPC fired — without simulating the post-Cancel `editing` snapshot or asserting that `document.activeElement` is the `[data-block-id='block-prior']` element. The actual focus-restoration $effect in EditorPanel.svelte:186-193 is therefore unverified for this edge case.
suggestedRemediation: After dispatching the Cancel click, simulate the domain ack by `_emitState({ status: 'editing', currentNoteId: 'note-1', focusedBlockId: 'block-prior', isDirty: true, isNoteEmpty: false, lastSaveResult: null })` then `flushSync()`, then assert `document.activeElement?.getAttribute('data-block-id')` equals `'block-prior'`. CRIT-712 listing alone does not guarantee EC-EDIT-014 is verified; this is the only coverage referenced in the matrix.
